Preamble: Government of November 26, 1987 as no cases of plague have been reported in Maharashtra since 1953
The Plague Surveillance Team was shut down as a result of the decision. However, cases in Beed district in the state are rural.
The suspected plague was discovered in August / September 1994. A total of 634 cases have been reported in all the districts of the state.
Blood was found to be contaminated with the plague. Therefore, d. Plague control team on October 03, 1994
was re-established. Since then, no case of plague has been reported in the state. However,
As a precautionary measure, plague teams have been deployed in Beed, Latur, Osmanabad, Pune, Satara, Solapur districts.
Testing of rats and fleas is being done regularly. A total of 17 posts are sanctioned for this team.
Out of these, 14 posts have become permanent and 3 posts are temporary. These 3 Temporaries in the Plague Control Program
Date 01.09.2025 to date 28.02.2026 under the Government Decision dated 06.10.2025 in respect of posts
